summary: Outline is broadly disciplined about citations. CB topic codes (CHA 8.7 - 8.12) all appear in the allowed reference. AP-CED conventions for unit names and topic naming match. Two specific external claims could not be verified against the allowed reference and warrant the lesson-writer's attention; one rhetorical claim about exam frequency is not in the AP CED and should be softened.

issues.description: Claim: 'usually one full FRQ per exam' (referring to volumes). The AP CED publishes per-unit weighting (Unit 8 is 10-15% of MC) but does NOT guarantee one volumes FRQ per exam. This is a marketing-flavored stat not in the allowed reference. Soften to 'a recurring AP free-response setup' or 'frequently tested on Section II.'

Claim: 'OpenStax Calculus Volume 2 sections 6.2 (volumes by slicing) and 6.3 (Disc & Washer Method).' Not in the allowed reference. The lesson-writer should double-check OpenStax's actual section numbering for the disc/washer content — Module 10's path slide references OpenStax sections 6.1 and 6.2, so a continuation to 6.2 / 6.3 is plausible but unverified. Common public OpenStax Calc Vol 2 numbering places volumes-by-slicing in 6.2 and Cylindrical Shells in 6.3, with Disc/Washer often folded into 6.2. Suggest the lesson-writer either confirm exact section titles or use a softer reference: 'OpenStax Calculus Volume 2, chapter 6 — volumes.'
'Unit 8b' is a GIIS internal partition of CB's Unit 8 (matches Module 10's 'Unit 8a'). Not an official CB designation but consistent with the prior module. Acceptable as a GIIS internal convention.

Claim: next module is 'Module 12 — Differential Equations and Slope Fields.' Not in the allowed reference. This is a curriculum-roadmap claim and should be verified against ROADMAP.md or the AP Calc AB module sequence. Flagging only because no source was given.
